Add execution layer documentation for the agent that implements plans:

redirected packets now count in per-interface TX stats.

commit.message:
fix(desktop): properly extract and render QR SVG with explicit size and white background

Signal's QR SVG has viewBox 0 0 16 16 with no explicit size and no
background. Add width=800 height=800 and a white rect background
before converting to PNG.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sync readiness reset + hold timer election wakeup (#133, #134)

#133: Reset syncReady=false on total peer disconnect (both fabrics down).
Re-set true only after fresh BulkEnd. Individual fabric failover
(fab0→fab1) does not reset readiness.

#134: Schedule time.AfterFunc at ReadySince + takeoverHoldTime to
re-run election. Cancel on readiness loss. Prevents indefinite
secondary state when no other events trigger re-evaluation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: require ALL VRRP instances MASTER for RG activation (#132)

rg_active was set as soon as any single VRRP instance in the RG became
MASTER, exposing partially-transitioned ownership during failover when
one interface reaches MASTER before others.

Change reconcileLocked() from anyMasterLocked() to allMasterLocked()
so the state machine requires every VRRP instance in the RG to be
MASTER before activating. Gate watchVRRPEvents() side-effects
(rg_active, blackhole removal, services) on tr.Changed && tr.Active
so they only fire on the final activation transition.

The dual-inactive window fix is preserved: Secondary still defers
rg_active=false until VRRP BACKUP event (deactivation path unchanged).

commit.message:
fix: sync established session state on periodic sweep (#131)

The sweep only replicated sessions at creation time (Created >=
lastSweepTime), leaving established flows with stale TCP state,
timeouts, and last-seen timestamps on standby.

Broaden the filter to also include sessions with LastSeen >=
lastSweepTime, so any session with recent packet activity gets
re-synced. The receiver already overwrites existing sessions, so
no wire format changes are needed.

Remove the GLOBAL_CTR_SESSIONS_NEW fast-path skip since it only
tracked new session creation, not ongoing activity.

fix: IPVLAN overlay reconciliation + dual-fabric auto-detect (#127-#130)

#127: Reconcile IPVLAN addresses on reapply — add missing, remove stale
addresses instead of skipping when overlay already exists on correct parent.

#128: Clean up stale fab0/fab1 overlays during config transitions —
inline cleanup in applyConfig removes interfaces not in active set.

#129: Probe ARP/NDP on overlay interface (fab0/fab1), not parent —
neighbor learning uses overlay ifindex, FabricFwdInfo uses parent ifindex
for XDP redirect. monitorFabricState watches overlay link state too.

#130: Auto-detect both FabricInterface and Fabric1Interface from fab0/fab1
syntax. Derive Fabric1PeerAddress from /30 or /31 point-to-point CIDR.

feat(agent): sistema de cola para CSF/Opinión de todos los clientes

- loadRfcsFromFiel(): detecta todos los RFCs con FIEL completa en fiel/
- runScraperForRfc(rfc): ejecuta scraper_sat.js y detecta éxito por stdout
- processQueue(): procesa la cola secuencialmente (un browser a la vez)
  - Cada RFC tiene hasta 3 intentos externos (outer attempts)
  - Si falla → al final de la cola; si agota 3 intentos → se descarta
  - Los 3 intentos internos de CSF y 32-D ya estaban en scraper_sat.js
- POST /enqueue-all: carga todos los RFCs e inicia la cola
- GET  /queue-status: devuelve estado actual (running, pending, queue)

- Testar Conexão valida gcloud auth print-access-token em tempo real
- Persiste preferência junto com os demais tokens do usuário

Uso (após configurar na UI):

fix: fabric health + gRPC dual-address failover (#121, #122, #124, #125)

#121: Clear stale fabric_fwd entries when fabric path dies — write
zeroed FabricFwdInfo{Ifindex:0} so dataplane falls back to other fabric.

#122: Check oper-state before programming fabric_fwd — reject non-UP
interfaces and clear their entries immediately.

#124: Add netlink-driven refresh for fabric link/neighbor changes via
LinkSubscribe + NeighSubscribe. 30s ticker kept as safety net.

#125: Make peer gRPC/monitor path dual-address-aware — listen on both
fabric IPs, dial with fab0→fab1 fallback, refactor CLI peer dialing
to use shared dialPeer() helper.

fix(desktop): resize then threshold QR for cleaner module edges

Upscale with bilinear resize before thresholding so anti-aliased edges
are smooth before binarization, avoiding single-pixel noise artifacts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: per-fabric session sync connections, prevent flapping (#123)

Replace single mutable s.conn with per-fabric conn0/conn1 slots.
fab0 is always preferred; fab1 is used only when fab0 is down.

Key changes:
- Track connections per-fabric (conn0 for fab0, conn1 for fab1)
- activeConnLocked() picks conn0 if available, else conn1
- handleNewConnection() sets the right slot, only triggers BulkSync
  on first connection after total disconnect (not when adding
  redundant fabric path)
- Per-fabric fabricConnectLoop() replaces single rotating connectLoop
- handleDisconnect() identifies which fabric to clear by pointer
  equality, only marks fully disconnected when both fabrics are down
- writeMu still serializes ALL writes (unchanged, critical for sync)
- writeFull() still loops until all bytes sent (unchanged)
- ActiveFabric() reports which fabric carries traffic (0, 1, or -1)
- SyncStatsSnapshot.ActiveFabric for status display

commit.message:
feat(patterns): add mandatory audit trail for dismissed findings to pr-codex-bot (#357)

* feat(patterns): add mandatory audit trail for dismissed findings to pr-codex-bot

* feat(pr-codex-bot,cli-sub-agent): harden PR audit trail with fail-closed parsing and explicit comment posting

Add Step 8a / workflow step 19 that explicitly posts dismissed-finding
audit trail comments via `gh pr comment --body-file` with fail-closed
parsing: duplicate VERDICT markers, missing PR_COMMENT_START/END pairs,
and absent required sections all abort instead of silently proceeding.
CONFIRMED verdicts reroute to the fix step without posting a dismissal
comment.

Clarify audit trail scope: PR-page findings only (local pre-PR findings
must be fixed before PR creation). Add 4 integration tests covering
dismissed, confirmed, malformed, and duplicate-verdict paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(desktop): upscale QR to 800x800 8-bit grayscale so it survives Signal's JPEG recompression

The 300x300 1-bit QR screenshots were unscannable after Signal re-compressed
them as JPEG when sending as attachments. Now we upscale with nearest-neighbor
sampling to 800x800 and output as 8-bit grayscale PNG, preserving sharp module
edges even after lossy recompression. Also bumped blank-detection threshold
from 2000 to 5000 bytes to match the larger output.

feat(graph): TF-IDF weighted Jaccard + ego-network + community detection

Backend:
- narrative_processor: replace plain Jaccard with TF-IDF weighted Jaccard
  (ENTITY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD 0.30→0.10 with IDF; safe fallback to plain Jaccard
  at 0.30 when entity_idf view not yet available)
- _load_entity_idf: loads IDF weights once per batch from materialized view
- database: add refresh_entity_idf() (CONCURRENTLY, fallback to blocking)
- process_narratives: refresh entity_idf before processing batch
- migrations/015: entity_idf materialized view + community_id column +
  updated v_active_storylines view to include community_id

API (stories.py):
- /stories/graph: min_edge_weight param (default 0.35) + 1h in-memory cache
- /stories/{id}/network: new ego-network endpoint (all neighbors, min_weight=0.05)
- StorylineNode schema: add community_id field

Community detection:
- scripts/compute_communities.py: Louvain with fixed seed, community 0=largest
- daily_pipeline: add community_detection step (continue_on_failure=True)
- requirements.txt: add python-louvain==0.16

Frontend (StorylineGraph):
- Physics: warmupTicks=300, cooldownTicks=0, d3AlphaDecay=0.05 (frozen layout)
- Ego-network UX: click node → useEgoNetwork → dim non-neighbors (alpha 0.08),
  highlight ego edges in orange, reset on second click
- Community colors: 8-color COMMUNITY_COLORS palette keyed on community_id
- GraphNode interface + graphData useMemo now include community_id
- types/stories.ts: StorylineNode + EgoNetworkResponse types
- hooks/useStories.ts: useEgoNetwork hook (SWR, no polling)

commit.message:
fix: mark DPDK fabric redirect as unsupported (#126)

The DPDK dataplane programs fabric port IDs for inbound zone-encoded
MAC detection but has no equivalent of the BPF try_fabric_redirect()
and try_fabric_redirect_with_zone() helpers. Add slog.Warn calls in
UpdateFabricFwd/UpdateFabricFwd1 and clarify the limitation in
dpdk_worker/zone.c comments.

Closes #126

commit.message:
fix: embedded ICMP fabric redirect after NAT rewrite (split-RG)

In split-RG cluster, ICMP Time Exceeded from transit routers arrives
on the WAN node but must reach the LAN host on the peer node.
handle_embedded_icmp_v4/v6() was calling try_fabric_redirect() BEFORE
NAT rewrite — the peer received packets with SNAT'd embedded headers
and couldn't match them to any session, silently dropping them.

Fix: when FIB fails (UNREACHABLE/BLACKHOLE) for the original client,
set META_FLAG_KERNEL_ROUTE instead of attempting immediate fabric
redirect. xdp_forward's KERNEL_ROUTE path re-FIBs using post-NAT
packet headers (outer dst rewritten to original client IP), detects
UNREACHABLE, and fabric-redirects with correct headers.

Also change meta_flags assignment from = to |= to preserve
KERNEL_ROUTE flag when setting META_FLAG_EMBEDDED_ICMP.

Tested: split-RG MTR shows all intermediate hops (was 100% loss),
make test-failover 14/14 pass at 7.86 Gbps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address code review findings

- Fix critical bug: UpdateIssue was sending "labelIds" (replaces all labels)
  instead of "addedLabelIds" (adds labels) in GraphQL mutation input
- Fix corresponding tests to check "addedLabelIds" key instead of "labelIds"
- Fix strings.Split without TrimSpace for teamIDs/memberIDs in project_mutations.go,
  use splitComma() helper that already handles whitespace; remove unused "strings" import
- Fix --order-by flag help text: remove non-existent "manualOrder" value
  (PaginationOrderBy enum only accepts "createdAt" and "updatedAt")
- Fix issues_test.go filter tests to check RunE errors instead of silently ignoring them
- Fix README documentation errors:
  - projects create: --team ENG -> --team-ids <TEAM-ID>
  - issue comment subcommands: comment update/delete/resolve/unresolve ->
    comment-update/comment-delete/comment-resolve/comment-unresolve
  - favorites add: --issue/--project -> --type/--id
  - initiatives updates create: --initiative -> --initiative-id
  - git-automation states create: --branch-pattern -> --event
  - issues list example: --order-by priority -> --order-by updatedAt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
